examples.title: Defect Density Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate defect density for a software project
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateDefectDensity
INPUT: totalDefects, linesOfCode
OUTPUT: defectDensity

BEGIN
 // Convert lines of code to KLOC
 kloc = linesOfCode / 1000
 
 // Calculate Defect Density
 IF kloc > 0 THEN
 defectDensity = totalDefects / kloc
 ELSE
 defectDensity = 0
 ENDIF
 
 // Determine quality rating
 IF defectDensity < 1 THEN
 rating = "Excellent"
 ELSE IF defectDensity <= 5 THEN
 rating = "Good"
 ELSE IF defectDensity <= 10 THEN
 rating = "Average"
 ELSE
 rating = "Poor"
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN defectDensity, rating
END
examples.explanation: Calculates defect density by dividing total defects by thousand lines of code (KLOC). Returns density value and quality rating based on industry standards.

examples.title: PDCA Cycle Implementation
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the PDCA continuous improvement cycle
examples.code:
PROCEDURE PDCA_ImprovementCycle(problemArea)
BEGIN
 // Phase 1: PLAN
 currentState = AnalyzeCurrentProcess(problemArea)
 targetState = DefineTargetState(problemArea)
 improvementPlan = CreatePlan(currentState, targetState)
 
 // Phase 2: DO
 pilotResults = ImplementOnSmallScale(improvementPlan)
 
 // Phase 3: CHECK
 actualResults = MeasureResults(pilotResults)
 gapAnalysis = Compare(actualResults, targetState)
 
 // Phase 4: ACT
 IF gapAnalysis.IsAcceptable() THEN
 StandardizeChanges(pilotResults)
 DocumentLessonsLearned()
 ELSE
 // Restart cycle with lessons
 revisedPlan = RevisePlan(improvementPlan, gapAnalysis)
 PDCA_ImprovementCycle(revisedPlan)
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN "Improvement cycle completed"
END
examples.explanation: Demonstrates the four phases of the PDCA cycle for continuous quality improvement. The cycle repeats until acceptable results are achieved.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - depends on number of iterations

examples.title: Quality Gate Check System
examples.description: Algorithm to evaluate if software meets quality criteria at each development phase
examples.code:
ALGORITHM QualityGateCheck(projectPhase, metrics)
INPUT: currentPhase, collectedMetrics
OUTPUT: pass/fail status

BEGIN
 thresholdTable = LoadQualityThresholds()
 
 CASE currentPhase OF
 "Requirements":
 reqMetrics = metrics[completeness, consistency]
 minThreshold = thresholdTable["Requirements"]
 result = CheckAgainstThreshold(reqMetrics, minThreshold)
 
 "Design":
 designMetrics = metrics[reviewCoverage, complexity]
 minThreshold = thresholdTable["Design"]
 result = CheckAgainstThreshold(designMetrics, minThreshold)
 
 "Coding":
 codeMetrics = metrics[defectDensity, codeReviewFindings]
 minThreshold = thresholdTable["Coding"]
 result = CheckAgainstThreshold(codeMetrics, minThreshold)
 
 "Testing":
 testMetrics = metrics[testCoverage, passRate]
 minThreshold = thresholdTable["Testing"]
 result = CheckAgainstThreshold(testMetrics, minThreshold)
 ENDCASE
 
 IF result.allPassed THEN
 RETURN "PASS - Proceed to next phase"
 ELSE
 RETURN "FAIL - Address quality issues"
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: Implements quality gates that software must pass at each development phase. Uses predefined thresholds for various quality metrics to decide if the project can proceed.
